evals.expected_output: Deep structured UX review of Linear covering IA, navigation, primary/secondary capabilities, critical flows including onboarding and issue creation, UX patterns, and product strategy notes

assertions.description: All 10 sections are present: Product Overview, Information Architecture, Primary Capabilities, Secondary Capabilities, Critical User Flows, UX Patterns, Onboarding Deep Dive, Product Strategy, UX Strengths, UX Gaps
Uses real Linear feature/screen names (e.g. Inbox, Cycles, Triage, My Issues, Teams, Views) rather than generic descriptions
Critical user flows section describes at least 2 flows with sequential steps, not just summary paragraphs
Section 8 (Product Strategy) connects UX decisions to strategic intent — explains *why* the product is designed a certain way, not just *what* it does
UX Strengths and UX Gaps each name specific features or flows, not generic observations like 'the UI is clean' or 'onboarding could be better'

evals.prompt: Walk me through the UX of Notion
evals.expected_output: Deep structured UX review of Notion covering its flexible block-based IA, primary capabilities like pages and databases, secondary capabilities, critical flows, UX patterns, and product strategy notes on flexibility vs complexity trade-offs

assertions.description: All 10 sections are present: Product Overview, Information Architecture, Primary Capabilities, Secondary Capabilities, Critical User Flows, UX Patterns, Onboarding Deep Dive, Product Strategy, UX Strengths, UX Gaps
Uses real Notion feature/screen names (e.g. Blocks, Pages, Databases, Gallery/Table/List/Board/Calendar views, Sidebar, Templates) rather than generic descriptions
Critical user flows section describes at least 2 flows with sequential steps, not just summary paragraphs
Section 8 (Product Strategy) connects UX decisions to strategic intent — e.g. addresses the flexibility vs. complexity trade-off and what audience Notion is optimizing for
UX Strengths and UX Gaps each name specific features or flows, not generic observations

evals.prompt: Break down the UX of Loom
evals.expected_output: Deep structured UX review of Loom covering its simple recording-focused IA, primary and secondary capabilities, the core recording and sharing flow, viewer experience, and product strategy notes on async communication

assertions.description: All 10 sections are present: Product Overview, Information Architecture, Primary Capabilities, Secondary Capabilities, Critical User Flows, UX Patterns, Onboarding Deep Dive, Product Strategy, UX Strengths, UX Gaps
Uses real Loom feature/screen names (e.g. My Library, Spaces, Loom Chrome Extension, recorder toolbar, viewer page, reactions, comments, CTAs) rather than generic descriptions
Critical user flows section covers both the recording flow AND the viewer/recipient flow with sequential steps
Section 8 (Product Strategy) connects UX decisions to async communication strategy — e.g. why friction is minimized in recording, how the viewer experience drives sharing virality
UX Strengths and UX Gaps each name specific features or flows, not generic observations
